mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in real savings and decreases in the federal funds rate signal expanding household liquidity and accommodative monetary policy, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in the same direction, or IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening real economic activity and corporate output capacity, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or signal direction reverses in >50% of expansionary observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and rising consumer spending capacity, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, the housing sector contraction signals broader demand weakness and credit stress, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 1200.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real rate tightening and eroding purchasing power, jointly suppressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.0% annually, or IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive returns when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or signal accuracy drops below 55% in recovery regimes.
